$comment: Community-template (per-community D1 shard) migrations that THIS API commit requires.
This manifest travels with the API commit, so the web release can ask a precise
question before deploying: 'does every live community shard satisfy the schema
requirements of the API I am about to pin?'
It exists because nothing used to gate that, and it broke production twice:
1124 -> async post publish broken on 25 shards
1127 -> every publish failed: 'no such column: story_ip_metadata_uri'
1126 -> Study/Karaoke attempts rolled back when rewards were enabled on older shards
WHEN YOU ADD A MIGRATION THAT NEW API CODE READS OR WRITES, ADD IT HERE.
If the code path runs regardless of feature flags, it is 'unconditional'.
If it only runs behind a flag bundle, declare it under 'features' instead —
then the fleet only has to satisfy it when that feature is being enabled.
1126 is intentionally unconditional because rewards are already enabled in staging and
a missing outbox turns a reward configuration drift into a Study/Karaoke outage.
1133 is positively attested by its namespace_role column, replacement indexes, and the absence of the dropped legacy index.

1143_lyrics_language.sql.rationale: Schema-tolerant Stage 1b rollout. Runtime reads are restricted to the audited projection/serialization path, and shards without all six columns receive NULL/false compatibility values. This is temporary and must not become an indefinite policy class.
1143_lyrics_language.sql.promotion_condition: Promote to unconditional only after the fleet reconciles as 100 applied + 1 quarantined DB_CMTY_0092, DB_CMTY_0092 is resolved through its quarantine process, and 1143 is independently verified in both schema_migrations and physical column presence across the resulting full live denominator. Stage 2 remains blocked until this promotion closes.

1139_story_registration_durable_request..rationale: Core prepares durable_request_json for persist-and-replay Story registration, but this API commit does not read or write that column. Require it when the durable registration runtime lands.
